preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] to the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] is a process of devoicing. This change frequently occurs in word-final positions (neutralization) or through assimilation to adjacent voiceless sounds.

examples.note: Final-obstruent devoicing where voiced affricates become voiceless word-finally.

sources: Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
